name: Upstate Telco
description_short: Upstate Telco Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving Gloversville, NY with savings accounts, loans, and financial services.
description_long: Upstate Telco Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union based in Gloversville, New York, operating under NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) insurance. The organization has been serving its membership with traditional credit union products and services, maintaining a physical branch location at 137 Harrison Street with regular business hours and phone/email support. As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, the credit union operates for the benefit of its members rather than external shareholders, a fundamental distinction from commercial banks. The institution maintains active community engagement, including scholarship programs and financial literacy resources like FAFSA filing assistance. Upstate Telco Federal Credit Union offers a range of financial products including share accounts (savings), share certificates (similar to CDs), personal loans, and online banking services accessible to members. The credit union provides both existing member services and new membership application processes, with loan application available to qualified members. They maintain a scholarship program with awards offered in 2026 and distribute seasonal newsletters with financial information. The institution has partnered with Sallie Mae for student loan products and provides financial aid guidance resources. What distinguishes this credit union is its hyperlocal focus in upstate New York with direct personal service during limited business hours (Monday-Thursday 9 AM-3 PM, Friday 8 AM-3 PM), emphasizing relationship banking over digital-only services. They proactively communicate security protocols and fraud prevention, explicitly stating they will never request confidential information via email. An honest assessment reveals that this is a small, community-focused institution with limited geographic reach and business hours that may not accommodate all members. The website provides basic information but lacks detailed product descriptions, rates, fee schedules, or comprehensive online service comparisons. Members seeking 24/7 digital banking, extensive branch networks, or competitive product comparisons may find limitations compared to larger financial institutions.
services: FAFSA filing assistance and financial aid resources
Holiday closures calendar and business hour scheduling
pros: NCUA-insured member accounts providing federal protection up to $250,000 per member
Not-for-profit structure means earnings benefit members rather than external shareholders
Scholarship program demonstrating community commitment to member education
Explicit security protocols and anti-fraud communication protecting member data
Online banking platform available for member account access
Personal service with direct phone support at (518) 773-6548 or toll-free (800) 324-8870
Partnership with Sallie Mae providing student loan options and financial aid resources
cons: Limited business hours (closes at 3 PM weekdays, 2:45 PM transaction cutoff) restrict member access
Single physical location in Gloversville, NY limits geographic accessibility for non-local members
Website lacks transparent rate information, fee schedules, and detailed product specifications
No clear information about minimum account balances, loan terms, or qualification requirements
Limited digital service offerings compared to larger financial institutions
best_for: Local Gloversville, NY residents and nearby upstate New York employees seeking relationship banking
Members prioritizing not-for-profit, community-focused financial institutions over commercial banks
Students and families interested in scholarship opportunities and financial aid guidance
Individuals seeking personal loans and savings products with federal NCUA insurance protection
diagnosis: Upstate Telco Federal Credit Union is best for local upstate New York residents who value community-focused, not-for-profit banking with member-centric benefits and personal service. The primary caveat is limited accessibility outside Gloversville and restricted business hours that may not suit members requiring extended or 24/7 digital services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
